title: Editas Medicine Reports Updated Clinical Data from the RUBY Trial of Reni-cel in Patients with Severe Sickle Cell Disease
text: Editas Medicine presented updated safety and efficacy data from the RUBY trial showing 28 patients treated with renizgamglogene autogedtemcel (reni-cel) for severe sickle cell disease. At data cutoff October 29, 2024, patients had median follow-up of 9.5 months post-infusion with 11 patients exceeding one year observation. Efficacy results showed 27 of 28 patients were free of vaso-occlusive events post-treatment. Mean total hemoglobin improved from 9.8 g/dL at baseline to 13.8 g/dL at month 6. Mean fetal hemoglobin reached 48.1% by month 6, well above anti-sickling thresholds. F-cell percentage sustained above 90% from month 4 through final follow-up. All hemolysis markers improved or normalized by month 6 including reticulocyte count, bilirubin, LDH, and haptoglobin. Quality of life improvements observed in pain, physical function, and social activities domains. Safety profile consistent with myeloablative conditioning with busulfan, with all 27 evaluable patients achieving successful engraftment. Median neutrophil engraftment at 23 days and platelet engraftment at 25 days. Two serious adverse events assessed as possibly treatment-related.
